articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Raphaël Guerreiro (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! FC Bayern München 1, FC St. Pauli Hamburg 0. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Michael Olise.
Goal! FC Bayern München 1, FC St. Pauli Hamburg 1. Elias Saad (FC St. Pauli Hamburg) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Manolis Saliakas with a cross.
Michael Olise (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
First Half ends, FC Bayern München 1, FC St. Pauli Hamburg 1.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. João Palhinha replaces Leon Goretzka.
Second Half begins FC Bayern München 1, FC St. Pauli Hamburg 1.
João Palhinha (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! FC Bayern München 2, FC St. Pauli Hamburg 1. Leroy Sané (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Michael Olise.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Hiroki Ito replaces Raphaël Guerreiro.
Substitution, FC St. Pauli Hamburg. Connor Metcalfe replaces Adam Dzwigala because of an injury.

Goal! FC Bayern München 3, FC St. Pauli Hamburg 1. Leroy Sané (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Harry Kane following a fast break.
Substitution, FC St. Pauli Hamburg. Oladapo Afolayan replaces Elias Saad.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Serge Gnabry replaces Jamal Musiala.
Substitution, FC St. Pauli Hamburg. Lars Ritzka replaces Manolis Saliakas.
Substitution, FC St. Pauli Hamburg. Abdoulie Ceesay replaces Danel Sinani.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Josip Stanisic replaces Konrad Laimer.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Thomas Müller replaces Harry Kane.

Goal! FC Bayern München 3, FC St. Pauli Hamburg 2. Lars Ritzka (FC St. Pauli Hamburg) left footed shot from outside the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by David Nemeth following a fast break.
Second Half ends, FC Bayern München 3, FC St. Pauli Hamburg 2.

article.story:
Bayern Munich's Leroy Sané scored twice in the second half as the Bundesliga leaders were made to work hard for a 3-2 win over visitors St. Pauli on Saturday to restore their six-point gap at the top going into the last seven matchdays.

Bayern, who face Inter Milan in their Champions League quarterfinal, first leg on April 8, have 65 points, with second-placed Bayer Leverkusen, 3-1 winners at home to VfL Bochum on Friday, on 59.

"We have to continue and we are one game closer. We have to win another seven games," Bayern coach Vincent Kompany said.

But there was some bad news for the league leaders with defender Hiroki Ito, who came on in the 58th minute, suffering a foot injury late in the game. The Japan defender broke the same foot back in July and had only recently come back.

Bayern are already without injured defenders Dayot Upamecano and Alphonso Davies.

"He (Ito) felt the same foot where he had the problems this season so that's not great for us," Kompany said. "The extent of what he has is a bit difficult to say right now. Let's not jump to conclusions but that's what it was."
